item.title: <Link Rel="apple-Touch-Icon" Sizes="180x180" Href="/Apple-Touch-Icon.png"> <Link Rel="icon" Type="image/Png" Sizes="32x32" Href="/Favicon-32x32.png"> <Link Rel="icon" Type="image/Png" Sizes="16x16" Href="/Favicon-16x16.png"> <Link Rel="manifest" Href="/Manifest.json"> <Link Rel="mask-Icon" Href="/Safari-Pinned-Tab.svg" Color="#5bbad5"> <Meta Name="theme-Color" Content="#ffffff">
Politics, Politicians and What everybody knows
item.description: Politics, political news and anything related to the so-called new world (supposed to be the better one) simply suck especially if you understand what's going on. Actually it only sucks if you understand what's going on, for instance if you're reading that the U.S. bombarded the Red Cross headquarters in Afghanistan or Aljazira headquarters because they're position for Al-Qaeda terrorists, well that's really a good news, Al Jazeera should only blame themselves.

item.title: “Connection” and “Meant-To-Be”
item.description: 2 concepts that seem really nice and agreeable, but I find are psychically terrible, are “connection” and “meant-to-be”. My issue with the concept of connection is that it requires equally the notion of disconnection, which I find logically leads to mass-extinction-production as suicide-bomber-ideology. The idea we are disconnected - which is different from the perspective that we are alienated (with connection/disconnection being a mode of alienation) - and must acquire connection, religious or spiritual or technological, I find to be a psychic trap, like a hamster wheel that never goes anywhere.

item.title: Book Review: Humiliated and Insulted by Fyodor Dostoevsky
item.description: The Insulted and Humiliated by Fyodor Dostoyevsky My rating: 4 of 5 stars This is my re-reading of “The Insulted and Injured” as part of my attempt to complete the complete work of Feydor Dostoyevsky. I had read the novel for a very long time, when I began reading Dostoevsky, and at that time I did not have the literary interest that makes me search about this novel, and I get enough for saying: It is a good novel.

item.title: Carl Schmidt: Politics, Conflict and War (Arabic)
item.description: لخيص حياة كارل شميدت الطويلة سوف يبدو من الضرورة أن نتوّخى الحذر بخصوص الدعاية السياسية لخصومه ومؤيّديه والتعامل معها أيضا، هذا على الأقل بخصوص حياته المثيرة للجدل. وُلِد شميدت بضواحي مدينة بليتنبارغ الألمانية في 11 جويلية 1888 لعائلة فرانكو-ألمانية كاثوليكية تنحدر من وادي موسيل ((The Moselle Valley، درس القانون بجامعات برلين، ميونيخ وستراسبورغ، متخرِّجا سنة 1915. في سنة 1921 صار أستاذا بغريفسفالد، وهنا نشر كتابه “الدكتاتورية”(Die Diktatur)، تبعه عمل مؤثّر حول موضوع “الثيولوجيا السياسية” سنة 1922، وذلك حينما انتقل شميدت إلى جامعة بون.

item.title: Book Review: The Crowd: A Study of the Popular Mind by Gustave Le Bon (Arabic)
item.description: سيكولوجية الجماهير by Gustave Le Bon My rating: 1 of 5 stars فرويد، مارتين شاركو، هما الاتنين فقط الي كتاباتهم البدائية في السيكولوجي لسا عايشة لحد دلوقتي، قدرت أخد - مع الوقت - نظرة تخيلية لشكل المجتمع العلمي القديم (القرن الثامن-التاسع عشر) قبل ما تتقلس الـclasses الكبرى وتتيح للطبقات المتوسطة إنها تشارك في الانتاج العلمي (بعد توزْع نسبة من الثروات).. المهم إن كريجولار ريدير تفرعت من الاتنين دول لباقي كبار المنتجين في البحث العلمي في القرن ده، جيمس فريزر، سبنسر، لانج، وأكيد لوبون.

item.title: On The Critique of MBTI (Arabic)
item.description: **عن اختبار الشخصية.. On critique of the MBTI من سنتين شوفت أول ظهور لاختبار ماري بريجز (MBTI) في إحدى الندوات الي بيحضرها طبيب مشهور على اليوتيوب، وكان بيروج للاختبار بإنه طريقة حَسِنة لإختيار الناس الي بتتعامل معاهم ودليل جيد للتعامل مع الناس دول (عن طريق إنك تعرف نوع شخصيتهم وتعرف سمات الشخصية دي وبالتالي تقدر تحايلهم).. وطبعًا الاختبار بما إنه بالإنجلش وبيقدم نتايج زي INPT وما شابه، وهي أشياء محتاجه دراسة قليلة علشان تفهمها، أغلب الجمهور (وأغلب دي تزيد عن 99%) شافو إن الاختبار دا أكيد مستند لـ “علم”، وأصبحت لما بقول لصديق إن الكلام دا هبل وإنه مش INTJ ولا حاجه، يقولي إني غلطان أكيد وإن الاختبار دا معمول عليه دراسات ومنتشر في أمريكا وبلا بلا بلا.

item.title: Cinema
item.description: Read: Update About My Tracking Lists BOLD TEXT: Currently Watching. If title is check-boxed, it is already watched. \ Prefect Zamani barayé masti asbha (2000) (Review) Au Revoir les Enfants (1987) Three Colors (1993) Being There (1979) Amadeus (1984) Amiele (2001) Cinema Paradiso (1988) 3-Iron (2004) 12 Angry Men (1954) Exotica (1994) In the Name of the Father (1993) The Two Popes (2019) V for Vendetta (2005) Lolita (1955) Departed (2006) Good Shutter Island (2010) Room (2015) The Best Offer (2013) The Words (2012) The King’s Speech (2010) 127 Hours (2010) Incendies (2010) Inception (2010) Oldboy (2003) Fargo (1996) Scent of a Woman (1992) Trumbo (2017) The Last King of Scotland (2006) The Intouchables (2017) Anna Karenina (1997) Portrait of a Lady on Fire (2019) A Dangerous Method (2011) A Beautiful Mind (2001) Memories of Murder (2003) I Saw the Devil (2010) The Handmaiden (2016) Captain Fantastic (2016) Joker (2019) American Psycho (2001) No Country For Old Man (2007) The Shawshank Redemption (1994) Léon: The Professional (1994) The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) Catch Me If You Can (2002) Parasite (2019) Forgotten (2017) The White Tiger (2021) The Dark Knight (2008) Requiem for a Dream (2000) American Beauty (1999) God on Trial (2008) Lost in translation (2003) Godfather (1991) Homes 99 (2014) The Platform (2019) The Yacoubian Building (2006) Greenbook (2018) The Piano (1993) Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter… and Spring (2003) Sympathy for Mr.

item.title: Update About My Tracking Lists
item.description: I figured out that whenever I can't hide some post from my feed page, because the dumb hugo developers disabled the option `hide` from the meta menu, and because I'm too lazy to implement it by myself.. I can just make the post date to 1900s so it will be in the bottom of the stack or the last page, it is not hidden yeah but who the hell will read the last page in my blog.

item.title: Books
item.description: Read: Update About My Tracking Lists The Full List Italics bold is an outstanding one. Literature and Fiction So, probably this list won’t be updated any more. 01 Nov, 2021. As this is simplified list, these are only what I really recommend, check the full list. Manfred (Byron) The Brothers Karamazov Children of the Alley (أولاد حارتنا) A Streetcar Named Desire Cat on a Hot Tin Roof Orpheus Descending The Misunderstanding (Camus) Umm Sa’d Men in the Sun (Kanafani) القميص المسروق الزيتون لا يموت The Idiot Crime and Punishment Suddenly Last Summer One Arm and Other Stories Marx in Soho: A Play on History The Dream of a Ridiculous Man The Insulted and Humiliated (Review) One Hundred Years of Solitude (Review) The Death of Ivan Ilych Philosophy An inequilateral note about reading philosophy; the vast majority of philosophy books is not worth reading, in fact it’s painful to read it, which is a thing I realized too late (~1 year ago, thanks to a friend of mine allowed me to read his dissertation in Obscurantism of philosophers), a lot of philosophy books I’ve read in the full list was useful but not really that ‘useful’, this is not the best place to explain why, but I’ll talk about this in the feature Inshaa' Allah.

item.title: Books
item.description: Read: Update About My Tracking Lists More simplified list Bold is currently reading Italics bold is an outstanding one. Literature and Fiction This section will not be updated anymore. Prefect Manfred (Byron) A Streetcar Named Desire The Kite Runner Lolita Suddenly Last Summer The Brothers Karamazov Umm Sa’d Children of the Alley Cat on a Hot Tin Roof Crime and Punishment The Idiot Orpheus Descending Marx in Soho: A Play on History One Arm and Other Stories The Misunderstanding (Camus) القميص المسروق الزيتون لا يموت Marx in Soho Good The Dream of a Ridiculous Man Chronicle of a Death Foretold The Unbearable Lightness of Being Poor Folk (Dostoevsky) In the Penal Colony Men in the Sun (Kanafani) Animal Farm Karnak Kafe The Insulted and Humiliated (Review) Love in the Time of Cholera One Hundred Years of Solitude (Review) Adrift on the Nile The Overcoat (Gogol) The Land of Sad Oranges (Kanafani) The Bet and Other Stories The Death of Ivan Ilych The Sorrows of Satan (Corelli) The Picture of Dorian Gray 1984 The Festival of Insignificance When Nietzsche Wept The Death of a Government Clerk The Silent Patient (Review) The End of the Fucking World (Comics) Season of Migration to the North The Inspector General Oscar And The Lady In Pink No One Sleeps in Alexandria يوم غائم في البر الغربي Sucks The Metamorphosis Hidden Figures War and Peace Wind, Sand and Stars (Review) Testaments Betrayed: An Essay in Nine Parts A Tale of Two Cities The Hunchback of Notre-Dame Sundays in August To Read Pope Joan: A Novel A Hero of Our Time (Lermontov) Death with Interruptions Perfume: The Story of a Murderer Prljave rukea (Kazantzakis) Christ Recrucified Nausea (Sartre) Confession (Gorky) Philosophy & Anthropology An inequilateral note about reading philosophy; the vast majority of philosophy books is not worth reading, in fact it’s painful to read it, which is a thing I realized too late (~1 year ago, thanks to a friend of mine allowed me to read his dissertation in Obscurantism of philosophers), a lot of philosophy books I’ve read in the full list was useful but not really that ‘useful’, this is not the best place to explain why, but I’ll talk about this in the feature Inshaa' Allah.
